practices.title: Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems).

objectives.text: Authorized users of the system are identified.
Processes acting on behalf of authorized users are identified.
Devices (and other systems) authorized to connect to the system are identified.
System access is limited to authorized users.
System access is limited to processes acting on behalf of authorized users.
System access is limited to authorized devices (including other systems).

practices.title: Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute.

objectives.text: The types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute are defined.
System access is limited to the defined types of transactions and functions for authorized users.

practices.title: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations.

objectives.text: Information flow control policies are defined.
Methods and enforcement mechanisms for controlling the flow of CUI are defined.
Designated sources and destinations for CUI within the system and between interconnected systems are identified.
CUI flow controls are enforced.

practices.title: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malevolent activity without collusion.

objectives.text: The duties of individuals requiring separation are defined.
Responsibilities for duties that require separation are assigned to separate individuals.
Access privileges that enable individuals to execute separated duties are granted to separate individuals.

practices.title: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts.

objectives.text: Privileged accounts are identified.
Access to privileged accounts is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege.
Security functions are identified.
Access to security functions is authorized in accordance with the principle of least privilege.

practices.title: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.

objectives.text: Users required to administer privileged accounts are identified.
Administrators use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions.

practices.title: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs.

objectives.text: Privileged functions are defined.
Non-privileged users are prevented from executing privileged functions.
The execution of privileged functions is captured in audit logs.

practices.title: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts.

objectives.text: The means of limiting unsuccessful logon attempts is defined.
The defined limit of unsuccessful logon attempts is enforced.

practices.title: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with CUI rules.

objectives.text: Privacy and security notices required by CUI-specified rules are identified, consistent with applicable CUI rules.
Privacy and security notices are displayed to system users.

practices.title: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays after a period of inactivity.

objectives.text: The period of inactivity after which the system initiates a session lock is defined.
A session lock is initiated after the defined period of inactivity.
Access to the system requires the user to re-authenticate following a session lock.

practices.title: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition.

objectives.text: Conditions requiring a user session to be automatically terminated are defined.
A user session is automatically terminated after any of the defined conditions are met.

practices.title: Monitor and control remote access sessions.

objectives.text: Remote access sessions are permitted and the conditions for their authorization are defined.
Remote access sessions are monitored.
Remote access sessions are controlled.

practices.title: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

objectives.text: Cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions are identified.
Cryptographic mechanisms are implemented to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions.

practices.title: Route remote access via managed access control points.

objectives.text: Managed access control points are identified and implemented.
Remote access is routed through managed access control points.

practices.title: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and access to security-relevant information via remote access only for documented operational needs.

objectives.text: Privileged commands authorized for remote execution are identified.
Security-relevant information authorized to be accessed remotely is identified.
The execution of privileged commands via remote access is authorized only for documented operational needs.
Access to security-relevant information via remote access is authorized only for documented operational needs.

practices.title: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections.

objectives.text: Wireless access to the system is authorized prior to allowing such connections.

practices.title: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption.

objectives.text: Wireless access is protected through authentication.
Wireless access is protected through encryption.

practices.title: Control connection of mobile devices.

objectives.text: Mobile devices that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified.
The connection of mobile devices is authorized.
The connection of mobile devices is monitored and controlled.

practices.title: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms.

objectives.text: Mobile devices and mobile computing platforms that process, store, or transmit CUI are identified.
CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms is encrypted.

practices.title: Verify and control/limit connections to external systems.

objectives.text: Connections to external systems are identified.
The use of external systems is identified and documented.
Connections to external systems are verified.
Connections to external systems are controlled and limited.

practices.title: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems.

objectives.text: The use of portable storage devices on external systems is identified and documented.
Limits on the use of portable storage devices on external systems are implemented.

practices.title: Control CUI posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.

objectives.text: Individuals authorized to post or process information on publicly accessible systems are identified.
Procedures to ensure CUI is not posted on or processed by publicly accessible systems are established.
A review process is implemented to ensure CUI is not posted or processed on publicly accessible systems.
Content on publicly accessible systems is reviewed to ensure it does not contain CUI.

practices.title: Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures.

objectives.text: Personnel with responsibilities for organizational systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities.
Personnel are made aware of applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of organizational systems.

practices.title: Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.

objectives.text: Information security responsibilities assigned to personnel are identified.
Personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security responsibilities.

practices.title: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential threats, including social engineering attacks.

objectives.text: Security awareness training includes recognizing and reporting potential threats, including social engineering attacks.
Security awareness training on reporting potential threats is provided to system users.

practices.title: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity.

objectives.text: The types of events to be logged are identified.
The content of audit records for identified events is defined.
Audit records for the identified events are created.
Audit records are retained for an adequate period to support monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting.

practices.title: Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions.

objectives.text: The content of audit records is sufficient to establish what events occurred.
Audit records uniquely establish who performed the actions that were logged.

practices.title: Review and update logged events.

objectives.text: The events requiring logging are reviewed and updated periodically.
The logging requirements are updated to reflect changes in the review.

practices.title: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure.

objectives.text: Personnel to be alerted in the event of an audit logging process failure are identified.
Alerts are generated to defined personnel in the event of an audit logging process failure.

practices.title: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes for investigation and response to indications of unlawful, unauthorized, suspicious, or unusual activity.

objectives.text: Audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes are coordinated.
Audit review, analysis, and reporting processes are used for investigation and response to indications of suspicious activity.

practices.title: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting.

objectives.text: An audit record reduction capability that processes audit information is provided.
A report generation capability that supports on-demand analysis and reporting is provided.

practices.title: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records.

objectives.text: The internal system clocks are used to generate time stamps for audit records.
The internal system clocks are synchronized to an authoritative source to generate accurate time stamps.

practices.title: Protect audit information and audit tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

objectives.text: Audit information is protected against unauthorized access.
Audit information is protected against unauthorized modification.
Audit tools are protected from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion.

practices.title: Limit management of audit logging to a subset of privileged users.

objectives.text: Privileged users authorized to manage audit logging are identified.
Management of audit logging is limited to the defined privileged users.

practices.title: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles.

objectives.text: Organizational systems and their components are inventoried.
Baseline configurations are established for organizational systems.
Baseline configurations are documented.
Baseline configurations are maintained throughout the system development life cycle.

practices.title: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems.

objectives.text: Security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems are established.
Security configuration settings are documented.
Security configuration settings are implemented.
Deviations from established security configuration settings are identified and documented.

practices.title: Track, review, approve, and log changes to organizational systems.

objectives.text: Changes to the system are identified.
Changes to the system are reviewed.
Changes to the system are approved.
Changes to the system are logged.

practices.title: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation.

objectives.text: The security impact of proposed changes to the system is analyzed prior to implementation.
The results of the security impact analysis are considered before implementing a change.

practices.title: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems.

objectives.text: Physical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are identified and documented.
Logical access restrictions associated with changes to the system are identified and documented.
Physical and logical access restrictions are enforced.

practices.title: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities.

objectives.text: Essential system capabilities necessary to support organizational missions are identified.
The system is configured to provide only the essential capabilities.

practices.title: Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services.

objectives.text: Nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services are identified.
Nonessential programs, ports, protocols, and services are restricted, disabled, or prevented.
The use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services is prohibited or restricted.

practices.title: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software or deny-all, permit-by-exception (whitelisting) policy to allow the execution of authorized software.

objectives.text: A policy to prevent the use of unauthorized software (blacklisting) or allow the execution of authorized software (whitelisting) is established.
Unauthorized software is identified and listed (blacklist) or authorized software is identified and listed (whitelist).
Only authorized software is permitted to execute.

practices.title: Control and monitor user-installed software.

objectives.text: A policy governing the installation of software by users is established.
User installation of software is controlled and monitored in accordance with the established policy.

practices.title: Identify system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices.

objectives.text: System users are identified.
Processes acting on behalf of users are identified.
Devices accessing the system are identified.

practices.title: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices, as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems.

objectives.text: The identity of each user is authenticated before allowing access to organizational systems.
The identity of each process acting on behalf of a user is authenticated before allowing access.
The identity of each device is authenticated before allowing access to organizational systems.

practices.title: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts.

objectives.text: Multifactor authentication is implemented for local access to privileged accounts.
Multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to privileged accounts.
Multifactor authentication is implemented for network access to non-privileged accounts.

practices.title: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

objectives.text: Replay-resistant authentication mechanisms are implemented for network access to privileged accounts.
Replay-resistant authentication mechanisms are implemented for network access to non-privileged accounts.

practices.title: Employ automated tools to support the management of user accounts.

objectives.text: Automated tools to support the management of user accounts are identified.
Automated tools are employed to support user account management.

practices.title: Disable identifiers after a defined inactivity period.

objectives.text: The period of inactivity after which an identifier is disabled is defined.
Identifiers are disabled after the defined period of inactivity.

practices.title: Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created.

objectives.text: Password complexity requirements are defined.
The minimum number of characters to be changed when new passwords are created is defined.
Password complexity requirements are enforced when new passwords are created.
The requirement to change a minimum number of characters is enforced when new passwords are created.

practices.title: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations.

objectives.text: The number of password generations for which reuse is prohibited is defined.
Reuse of passwords is prohibited for the defined number of generations.

practices.title: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password.

objectives.text: Temporary passwords for system logons are allowed.
Immediate change to a permanent password is required following system logon with a temporary password.

practices.title: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords.

objectives.text: Passwords are stored using cryptographic protection.
Passwords are transmitted using cryptographic protection.

practices.title: Obscure feedback of authentication information.

objectives.text: Authentication information is obscured during the authentication process.
Feedback of authentication information is hidden during the authentication process.

practices.title: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities.

objectives.text: An incident-handling capability for the system is established.
The incident-handling capability includes preparation activities.
The incident-handling capability includes detection and analysis activities.
The incident-handling capability includes containment, eradication, and recovery activities.

practices.title: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization.

objectives.text: Incidents are tracked.
Incidents are documented and reported to designated internal officials.
Incidents are reported to designated external authorities as appropriate.

practices.title: Test the organizational incident response capability.

objectives.text: The incident response capability is tested.
The results of incident response testing are used to improve the incident response capability.

practices.title: Perform maintenance on organizational systems.

objectives.text: Maintenance is performed on organizational systems.
Maintenance is performed using approved tools, techniques, and methods.

practices.title: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel for the conduct of system maintenance.

objectives.text: Maintenance personnel are controlled.
Tools used for maintenance are controlled.
Maintenance activities are supervised.

practices.title: Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of any CUI.

objectives.text: Equipment to be removed for off-site maintenance is identified.
Equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of CUI before removal.

practices.title: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in organizational systems.

objectives.text: Media containing diagnostic and test programs to be used in organizational systems are identified.
Media containing diagnostic and test programs are checked for malicious code before being used in organizational systems.

practices.title: Require MFA to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external networks and terminate such connections when nonlocal maintenance is complete.

objectives.text: Multifactor authentication is employed to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external networks.
Nonlocal maintenance sessions are terminated when maintenance is complete.

practices.title: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization.

objectives.text: Maintenance personnel without required access authorization are identified.
The maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization are supervised.

practices.title: Protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI, both paper and digital.

objectives.text: Paper media containing CUI is physically controlled.
Digital media containing CUI is physically controlled.
Media containing CUI is securely stored.

practices.title: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users.

objectives.text: Media containing CUI is identified.
Access to media containing CUI is limited to authorized users.

practices.title: Sanitize or destroy system media before disposal or reuse.

objectives.text: System media is sanitized or destroyed before disposal or reuse.
The media sanitization or destruction is consistent with the applicable security category.

practices.title: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations.

objectives.text: CUI markings are applied to media containing CUI.
Distribution limitations are applied to media containing CUI.

practices.title: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability for media during transport outside of controlled areas.

objectives.text: Access to media containing CUI during transport is controlled.
Accountability for media containing CUI is maintained during transport.
Media containing CUI is protected against unauthorized disclosure during transport.

practices.title: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI during transport unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

objectives.text: The types of CUI that require cryptographic protection during transport are identified.
Cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI are implemented during transport.

practices.title: Control the use of removable media on system components.

objectives.text: The use of removable media on system components is identified and documented.
The use of removable media on system components is controlled.

practices.title: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner.

objectives.text: Portable storage devices in use are identified and their owners established.
The use of portable storage devices with no identifiable owner is prohibited.

practices.title: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations.

objectives.text: Backup CUI is identified.
The confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations is protected.

practices.title: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI.

objectives.text: Criteria for screening individuals prior to authorizing access to the system are defined.
Individuals are screened prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI.

practices.title: Ensure that CUI is protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.

objectives.text: CUI is protected during and after personnel terminations.
CUI is protected during and after personnel transfers.
CUI access is terminated upon departure of personnel from the organization.

practices.title: Limit physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

objectives.text: Individuals authorized to have physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and operating environments are identified.
Physical access is limited to authorized individuals.
Physical access is managed.

practices.title: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational systems.

objectives.text: The physical facility is protected.
The physical facility is monitored.
Support infrastructure for organizational systems is protected and monitored.

practices.title: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.

objectives.text: Visitors are escorted in areas containing organizational systems and equipment.
Visitor activity is monitored.

practices.title: Maintain audit logs of physical access.

objectives.text: Audit logs of physical access are maintained.
Physical access audit logs are reviewed.

practices.title: Control and manage physical access devices.

objectives.text: Physical access devices are inventoried.
Physical access devices are controlled and managed.

practices.title: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.

objectives.text: Alternate work sites where CUI is processed, stored, or transmitted are identified.
Safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites are defined.
Safeguarding measures are enforced at alternate work sites.

practices.title: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals, resulting from the operation of organizational systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.

objectives.text: A risk assessment is conducted periodically.
The risk assessment identifies threats to organizational systems and CUI.
The risk assessment identifies vulnerabilities in organizational systems.

practices.title: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified.

objectives.text: Vulnerabilities in organizational systems are scanned periodically.
New vulnerabilities affecting organizational systems are identified.
Vulnerability scans are performed when new vulnerabilities are identified.

practices.title: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.

objectives.text: Vulnerabilities identified during scanning are remediated.
Vulnerabilities are remediated in accordance with risk assessments.

practices.title: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application.

objectives.text: Security controls in organizational systems are periodically assessed.
The security controls are assessed to determine if they are effective in their application.
The assessment results are documented.

practices.title: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems.

objectives.text: Plans of action are developed to correct identified deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities.
Plans of action are implemented.

practices.title: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls.

objectives.text: Security controls are monitored on an ongoing basis.
Ongoing monitoring results are used to determine the continued effectiveness of security controls.

practices.title: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems.

objectives.text: A system security plan is developed and documented.
The system security plan describes the system boundary.
The system security plan describes how security requirements are implemented.
The system security plan is periodically updated.

practices.title: Monitor, control, and protect communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems.

objectives.text: The external boundaries of the system are defined.
Key internal boundaries of the system are defined.
Communications at external and key internal system boundaries are monitored, controlled, and protected.

practices.title: Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security within organizational systems.

objectives.text: Security engineering principles are used in the design and implementation of the system's security architecture.
Software development techniques that promote effective security are employed.

practices.title: Separate user functionality from system management functionality.

objectives.text: User functionality is identified.
System management functionality is identified.
User functionality is separated from system management functionality.

practices.title: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

objectives.text: Shared system resources that could enable unauthorized information transfer are identified.
Unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources is prevented.

practices.title: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

objectives.text: Publicly accessible system components are identified.
Publicly accessible system components are physically or logically separated from internal networks via subnetworks.

practices.title: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception).

objectives.text: Network communications traffic is denied by default.
Network communications traffic is allowed only by exception (permit by exception).

practices.title: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously establishing connections with the system and other resources (i.e., split tunneling) unless the connection is established using an approved split tunneling policy.

objectives.text: Split tunneling for remote devices is identified and a policy is established.
Remote devices are prevented from simultaneously establishing connections with the system and other resources unless split tunneling is approved.

practices.title: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

objectives.text: Cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission are identified.
Cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI during transmission are implemented.

practices.title: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

objectives.text: The period of inactivity after which a network connection is terminated is defined.
Network connections are terminated at the end of sessions or after the defined period of inactivity.

practices.title: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for required cryptography employed in organizational systems.

objectives.text: Cryptographic key management requirements are established.
Cryptographic keys are generated, distributed, stored, accessed, and destroyed in accordance with requirements.
Cryptographic key management activities are documented.

practices.title: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

objectives.text: FIPS-validated cryptographic modules are identified.
FIPS-validated cryptography is employed to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

practices.title: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of use to present users.

objectives.text: Remote activation of collaborative computing devices is prohibited.
An indication is provided to users present when collaborative computing devices are in use.

practices.title: Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

objectives.text: Approved mobile code technologies are defined.
The use of mobile code is controlled and monitored.
Mobile code is controlled according to established policy.

practices.title: Control and monitor the use of VoIP technologies.

objectives.text: Authorized VoIP technologies are defined.
The use of VoIP technologies is controlled and monitored.
VoIP technologies are controlled according to established policy.

practices.title: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

objectives.text: The authenticity of communications sessions is protected.
Communications sessions are protected against hijacking and unauthorized disclosure.

practices.title: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.

objectives.text: CUI at rest is identified.
The confidentiality of CUI at rest is protected.

practices.title: Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws in a timely manner.

objectives.text: Flaws in the system are identified.
Identified flaws are reported.
Identified flaws are corrected in a timely manner.

practices.title: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organizational systems.

objectives.text: Malicious code protection mechanisms are employed at entry and exit points of the system.
Malicious code protection mechanisms are employed at workstations, servers, and mobile computing devices.
The malicious code protection mechanisms detect and quarantine malicious code.

practices.title: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response.

objectives.text: Security alerts and advisories are received from external organizations and information sources.
Alerts and advisories are acted upon in response to identified threats.

practices.title: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.

objectives.text: Malicious code protection mechanisms are updated when new releases are available.
Updates to malicious code protection mechanisms are tested.

practices.title: Perform periodic scans of organizational systems and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.

objectives.text: Periodic scans of organizational systems are performed.
Real-time scans of files from external sources are performed as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.
Malicious code is detected and eradicated.

practices.title: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

objectives.text: Inbound communications traffic is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
Outbound communications traffic is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.
Potential attacks and other indicators of compromise are detected.

practices.title: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems.

objectives.text: Authorized use of the system is defined.
Unauthorized use of the system is identified through monitoring and alerting.
